frameworks.name: feedforward / feedback synaptic balance
frameworks.one_line: gamma indexes feedforward and alpha indexes feedback synaptic activity

frameworks.name: contrast-invariant orientation tuning
frameworks.one_line: classical neurophysiology result the BOLD finding appears to violate

frameworks.name: neurovascular coupling of BOLD signal
frameworks.one_line: BOLD reflects mixture of feedforward and feedback synaptic input

core_question: Why does BOLD orientation tuning in early visual cortex depend on stimulus contrast when single-unit tuning is contrast-invariant?
key_assumptions: gamma-band power indexes feedforward synaptic activity
alpha-band power indexes feedback synaptic activity
BOLD signal reflects a mixture of feedforward and feedback input
manipulations: stimulus orientation across cardinal vs oblique axes
neural readout (BOLD vs EEG across frequency bands)
key_findings: V1 BOLD showed inverse-oblique tuning at high contrast and horizontal effect at low contrast
EEG orientation tuning was contrast-invariant but frequency-specific
gamma-band tuning showed inverse-oblique effect; alpha-band tuning showed horizontal effect
high-contrast BOLD matched gamma (FF) tuning; low-contrast BOLD matched alpha (FB) tuning
interpretations: contrast-dependent BOLD orientation tuning arises from reduced feedforward contribution at low contrast
BOLD shifts toward feedback orientation preferences when feedforward drive weakens
